lines.english: Lesson 42
lines.chinese: 第42课
lines.raw: Lesson 42 | 第42课

lines.english: Modern cavemen
lines.chinese: 现代穴居人
lines.raw: Modern cavemen | 现代穴居人

lines.english: With what does the writer compare the Gouffre Berger?
lines.chinese: 作者把贝杰深洞与什么作比较？
lines.raw: With what does the writer compare the Gouffre Berger? | 作者把贝杰深洞与什么作比较？

lines.english: Cave exploration, or pot-holing, as it has come to be known, is a relatively new sport.
lines.chinese: 洞穴探险，即人们所知的“探洞”，是一项比较新的运动。
lines.raw: Cave exploration, or pot-holing, as it has come to be known, is a relatively new sport. | 洞穴探险，即人们所知的“探洞”，是一项比较新的运动。

lines.english: Perhaps it is the desire for solitude or the chance of making an unexpected discovery that lures people down to the depths of the earth.
lines.chinese: 也许是出于对孤独的渴望，或是想作一次意想不到的发现，把人们引到地下去的。
lines.raw: Perhaps it is the desire for solitude or the chance of making an unexpected discovery that lures people down to the depths of the earth. | 也许是出于对孤独的渴望，或是想作一次意想不到的发现，把人们引到地下去的。

lines.english: It is impossible to give a satisfactory explanation for a pot-holer's motives.
lines.chinese: 想对探洞者的动机作出满意的解释是不可能的。
lines.raw: It is impossible to give a satisfactory explanation for a pot-holer's motives. | 想对探洞者的动机作出满意的解释是不可能的。

lines.english: For him, caves have the same peculiar fascination which high mountains have for the climber.
lines.chinese: 对于他来说，洞穴有着与高山对于登山者一样的奇异魅力。
lines.raw: For him, caves have the same peculiar fascination which high mountains have for the climber. | 对于他来说，洞穴有着与高山对于登山者一样的奇异魅力。

lines.english: They arouse instincts which can only be dimly understood.
lines.chinese: 它们唤起人们只能模模糊糊感知到的本能。
lines.raw: They arouse instincts which can only be dimly understood. | 它们唤起人们只能模模糊糊感知到的本能。

lines.english: Exploring really deep caves is not a task for the Sunday afternoon rambler.
lines.chinese: 勘探真正深的洞穴并非是周日下午散步者所能胜任的。
lines.raw: Exploring really deep caves is not a task for the Sunday afternoon rambler. | 勘探真正深的洞穴并非是周日下午散步者所能胜任的。

lines.english: Such undertakings require the precise planning and foresight of military operations.
lines.chinese: 这种活动需要像军事行动一样精确的计划和预见性。
lines.raw: Such undertakings require the precise planning and foresight of military operations. | 这种活动需要像军事行动一样精确的计划和预见性。

lines.english: It can take as long as eight days to rig up rope ladders and to establish supply bases before a descent can be made into a very deep cave.
lines.chinese: 在下到一个很深的洞穴之前，光是架设绳梯和建立补给站就可能需要长达8天的时间。
lines.raw: It can take as long as eight days to rig up rope ladders and to establish supply bases before a descent can be made into a very deep cave. | 在下到一个很深的洞穴之前，光是架设绳梯和建立补给站就可能需要长达8天的时间。

lines.english: Precautions of this sort are necessary, for it is impossible to foretell the exact nature of the difficulties which will confront the pot-holer.
lines.chinese: 这种预防是必要的，因为无法预知探洞者将会遇到的困难究竟是什么性质。
lines.raw: Precautions of this sort are necessary, for it is impossible to foretell the exact nature of the difficulties which will confront the pot-holer. | 这种预防是必要的，因为无法预知探洞者将会遇到的困难究竟是什么性质。

lines.english: The deepest known cave in the world is the Gouffre Berger near Grenoble.
lines.chinese: 世界上已知最深的洞穴是格勒诺布尔附近的贝杰深洞。
lines.raw: The deepest known cave in the world is the Gouffre Berger near Grenoble. | 世界上已知最深的洞穴是格勒诺布尔附近的贝杰深洞。

lines.english: It extends to a depth of 3, 723 feet.
lines.chinese: 它深达3723英尺。
lines.raw: It extends to a depth of 3, 723 feet. | 它深达3723英尺。

lines.english: This immense chasm has been formed by an underground stream which has tunnelled a course through a flaw in the rocks.
lines.chinese: 这个巨大的深渊是由一条地下河形成的，这条河在岩石的裂缝中开凿出一条通道。
lines.raw: This immense chasm has been formed by an underground stream which has tunnelled a course through a flaw in the rocks. | 这个巨大的深渊是由一条地下河形成的，这条河在岩石的裂缝中开凿出一条通道。

lines.english: The entrance to the cave is on a plateau in the Dauphine Alps.
lines.chinese: 洞口位于多芬阿尔卑斯山脉的一个高原上。
lines.raw: The entrance to the cave is on a plateau in the Dauphine Alps. | 洞口位于多芬阿尔卑斯山脉的一个高原上。

lines.english: As it is only six feet across, it is barely noticeable.
lines.chinese: 由于洞口只有6英尺宽，所以几乎难以察觉。
lines.raw: As it is only six feet across, it is barely noticeable. | 由于洞口只有6英尺宽，所以几乎难以察觉。

lines.english: The cave might never have been discovered had not the entrance been spotted by the distinguished French pot-holer, Berger.
lines.chinese: 如果不是杰出的法国探洞者贝杰发现了洞口，这个洞穴可能永远也不会被发现。
lines.raw: The cave might never have been discovered had not the entrance been spotted by the distinguished French pot-holer, Berger. | 如果不是杰出的法国探洞者贝杰发现了洞口，这个洞穴可能永远也不会被发现。

lines.english: Since its discovery, it has become a sort of pot-holers' Everest.
lines.chinese: 自从被发现以来，它已成为探洞者们的一种“珠穆朗玛峰”。
lines.raw: Since its discovery, it has become a sort of pot-holers' Everest. | 自从被发现以来，它已成为探洞者们的一种“珠穆朗玛峰”。

lines.english: Though a number of descents have been made, much of it still remains to be explored.
lines.chinese: 尽管已经进行过多次下降探险，但其中大部分仍有待勘探。
lines.raw: Though a number of descents have been made, much of it still remains to be explored. | 尽管已经进行过多次下降探险，但其中大部分仍有待勘探。

lines.english: A team of pot-holers recently went down the Gouffre Berger.
lines.chinese: 一队探洞者最近下到了贝杰深洞。
lines.raw: A team of pot-holers recently went down the Gouffre Berger. | 一队探洞者最近下到了贝杰深洞。

lines.english: After entering the narrow gap on the plateau,
lines.chinese: 他们进入高原上的那个狭窄的缝隙后，
lines.raw: After entering the narrow gap on the plateau, | 他们进入高原上的那个狭窄的缝隙后，

lines.english: they climbed down the steep sides of the cave until they came to a narrow corridor.
lines.chinese: 沿着洞穴陡峭的侧壁向下攀爬，直到来到一条狭窄的通道。
lines.raw: they climbed down the steep sides of the cave until they came to a narrow corridor. | 沿着洞穴陡峭的侧壁向下攀爬，直到来到一条狭窄的通道。

lines.english: They had to edge their way along this,
lines.chinese: 他们不得不侧身沿着这条通道前行，
lines.raw: They had to edge their way along this, | 他们不得不侧身沿着这条通道前行，

lines.english: sometimes wading across shallow streams, or swimming across deep pools.
lines.chinese: 有时涉过浅浅的小溪，有时游过深深的水潭。
lines.raw: sometimes wading across shallow streams, or swimming across deep pools. | 有时涉过浅浅的小溪，有时游过深深的水潭。

lines.english: Suddenly they came to a waterfall which dropped into an underground lake at the bottom of the cave.
lines.chinese: 突然，他们来到一个瀑布，瀑布落入洞穴底部的一个地下湖中。
lines.raw: Suddenly they came to a waterfall which dropped into an underground lake at the bottom of the cave. | 突然，他们来到一个瀑布，瀑布落入洞穴底部的一个地下湖中。

lines.english: They plunged into the lake, and after loading their gear on an inflatable rubber dinghy, let the current carry them to the other side.
lines.chinese: 他们跳入湖中，把装备装到一艘充气橡皮艇上，然后让水流把他们带到对岸。
lines.raw: They plunged into the lake, and after loading their gear on an inflatable rubber dinghy, let the current carry them to the other side. | 他们跳入湖中，把装备装到一艘充气橡皮艇上，然后让水流把他们带到对岸。

lines.english: To protect themselves from the icy water, they had to wear special rubber suits.
lines.chinese: 为了保护自己不受冰冷湖水的侵袭，他们不得不穿上特制的橡胶衣。
lines.raw: To protect themselves from the icy water, they had to wear special rubber suits. | 为了保护自己不受冰冷湖水的侵袭，他们不得不穿上特制的橡胶衣。

lines.english: At the far end of the lake, they came to huge piles of rubble which had been washed up by the water.
lines.chinese: 在湖的远端，他们遇到了被水冲刷而成的巨大碎石堆。
lines.raw: At the far end of the lake, they came to huge piles of rubble which had been washed up by the water. | 在湖的远端，他们遇到了被水冲刷而成的巨大碎石堆。

lines.english: In this part of the cave, they could hear an insistent booming sound
lines.chinese: 在洞穴的这部分，他们能听到一种持续不断的轰鸣声，
lines.raw: In this part of the cave, they could hear an insistent booming sound | 在洞穴的这部分，他们能听到一种持续不断的轰鸣声，

lines.english: which they found was caused by a small waterspout shooting down into a pool from the roof of the cave.
lines.chinese: 他们发现这是由一股小小的水柱从洞顶射入水潭所引起的。
lines.raw: which they found was caused by a small waterspout shooting down into a pool from the roof of the cave. | 他们发现这是由一股小小的水柱从洞顶射入水潭所引起的。

lines.english: Squeezing through a cleft in the rocks, the pot-holers arrived at an enormous cavern, the size of a huge concert hall.
lines.chinese: 探洞者们挤过岩石中的一道裂缝，来到一个巨大的洞穴，其大小相当于一个巨大的音乐厅。
lines.raw: Squeezing through a cleft in the rocks, the pot-holers arrived at an enormous cavern, the size of a huge concert hall. | 探洞者们挤过岩石中的一道裂缝，来到一个巨大的洞穴，其大小相当于一个巨大的音乐厅。

lines.english: After switching on powerful arc lights,
lines.chinese: 打开强光弧光灯后，
lines.raw: After switching on powerful arc lights, | 打开强光弧光灯后，

lines.english: they saw great stalagmites--some of them over forty feet high--rising up like tree-trunks to meet the stalactites suspended from the roof.
lines.chinese: 他们看到了巨大的石笋——其中一些高达40多英尺——像树干一样向上生长，与悬挂在洞顶的钟乳石相接。
lines.raw: they saw great stalagmites--some of them over forty feet high--rising up like tree-trunks to meet the stalactites suspended from the roof. | 他们看到了巨大的石笋——其中一些高达40多英尺——像树干一样向上生长，与悬挂在洞顶的钟乳石相接。

lines.english: Round about, piles of limestone glistened in all the colours of the rainbow.
lines.chinese: 四周，一堆堆的石灰石闪耀着彩虹般的光芒。
lines.raw: Round about, piles of limestone glistened in all the colours of the rainbow. | 四周，一堆堆的石灰石闪耀着彩虹般的光芒。

lines.english: In the eerie silence of the cavern, the only sound that could be heard was made by water which dripped continuously from the high dome above them.
lines.chinese: 在洞穴诡异的寂静中，唯一能听到的声音是头顶高高的拱顶不断滴落的水声。
lines.raw: In the eerie silence of the cavern, the only sound that could be heard was made by water which dripped continuously from the high dome above them. | 在洞穴诡异的寂静中，唯一能听到的声音是头顶高高的拱顶不断滴落的水声。
